DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-26-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: CROSS DEFENDANT HUGH KING DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to 1st, 2nd, 3rd, 4th, 5th, and 7th causes of action. Cross-complaint must allege whether the contract was oral or written and attach a copy to the cross-complaint. Cross-complainant must plead a statutory duty to support the 2nd cause of action. As to the 3rd and 4th causes of action, cross-complainant must plead a valid contract between cross-complainant and a third party, and plead damages. The claim of trespass is deficient because cross-complainant does not allege that cross-defendant did not have a right or interest in the subject property. The 7th cause of action is based upon the 1st and 2nd causes of action, and cross-complainant must allege a duty. Demurrer overruled as to 6th cause of action (nuisance.) (Moving party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
